title: Optimizing React Memoized Component Re-renders on Object Equality

statement.markdown: A React component is memoized to avoid unnecessary re-renders. However, due to a logical bug, the component still re-renders when the object properties change. Fix the bug to prevent unnecessary re-renders.
statement.inputFormat: An object with integer properties (e.g., `{ a: 1, b: 2 }`)
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the component should re-render (true) or not (false)
statement.notes: The object properties may change, but the object reference remains the same.

constraints.description: The maximum number of properties in the object
The maximum value of a property

examples.explanation: The object properties do not change, so the component should not re-render
The object properties change, so the component should re-render

hints.text: Check the object reference before comparing properties

editorial.title: Memoization and Object Equality
editorial.intuition: Memoization is used to avoid unnecessary re-renders, but it's essential to compare object references correctly
editorial.bruteForce: Comparing all properties individually can lead to inefficient re-renders
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the `JSON.stringify` method to compare object references
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The `JSON.stringify` method returns a string representation of the object, which can be compared for equality
